AI-generated software is becoming its own software supply chain. @cursor_ai, @Lovable, @Replit, @boltdotnew,@claudeai, @grok, agents, dApps, MCP tools, all of them are moving code from prompt to production faster than traditional security workflows can keep up. That creates a new requirement: security validation has to become model-native. Nullsec-S1 is being built for that layer. A security-specialized LLM that sits between AI-generated code and production, analyzing auth logic, secrets, env exposure, SQLi, SSRF, XSS, MCP/tool abuse, shell execution, dependency risk, wallet flows, and safe-code calibration. AI builds the software. Nullsec-S1 validates it.

Floot just hit $1M ARR. A year ago, @floothq didn't exist. We launched just over 6 months ago with a scrappy video and zero marketing budget. I’m really proud of what @edlooky and I have built with Floot - just the two of us (thanks to my wife for catching a candid of us hard at work). It was a lot of showing up every day and figuring things out as we go. Doing this while being a full-time dad definitely made things more interesting. Time was tight, priorities were real, and balance was difficult. But it made the wins feel that much more meaningful. This milestone feels less like an endpoint and more like a good signal that we’re on the right path. There’s still a lot we want to build, and I’m excited to keep growing from here. Grateful for everyone who’s been part of the journey so far.

Your website is now a mobile app. No rebuild. No extra code. Today we're launching mobile app export on @floothq - take any website you've built and export it directly as a native mobile app. Same code, zero changes. For those new here: Floot is a vibe coding platform where non-technical founders and business owners can build complete, production-grade apps end-to-end. Real databases, real backend, real hosting — not just pretty prototypes. And now, real mobile apps too. This feature is currently in preview, and we want early adopters to try it out. Comment "floot" below and I'll DM you a 30% discount code + 5,000 bonus credits to get started.

Most websites aren’t failing because they’re bad. They’re failing because search engines can’t see them. Today we launched Improved SEO Support on @floothq. You can now enable server-side rendering, set your primary domain, configure search metadata, and generate robots.txt + sitemap.xml directly inside Floot. Competitors like Lovable, Base44, and Replit still don’t support server-side rendering. With Floot, we’re focused on making sure the apps you build can actually be found. Comment “floot” and I’ll DM you a 30% code so you can try it out :)

Sometimes big milestones go unnoticed. While browsing our analytics, I realized @floothq quietly crossed a meaningful one: 1,000,000+ visitors across websites built with Floot in the last 90 days. That number represents real people using real products created by our builders. One of our biggest goals with Floot has always been to make a platform where you can build something that actually works. Seeing these numbers come together feels like proof that we’re heading in the right direction. A huge thank you to every builder who’s trusted Floot to bring their ideas to life. This is starting to feel real in a new way, and it’s only up from here.

I'm proud to share that @Glean has surpassed $300M ARR, just five months after crossing $200M and growing ~3x over the past 15 months. This is an exciting milestone for Glean, and it's a signal about where the enterprise AI market is heading. We’ve long believed the real challenge in enterprise AI is not access to models. It is grounding AI in how a company actually works: its people, knowledge, workflows, permissions, and systems. That’s even clearer now. The companies creating real value with AI are not just adopting better models. They are building systems that understand their business well enough to deliver reliable outcomes at scale. That is the real moat, and it is what we’ve been building at Glean: an unrivaled context layer for enterprise AI. That context has to work across the business, not just inside a single team or use case. We see that in how customers adopt Glean: more than 85% use it across five or more job functions. It also has to meet the security and governance demands of complex enterprises. We see that in who is choosing Glean: our Fortune 500 customer count nearly doubled year over year. And it has to make economic sense as usage grows. In our recent benchmark with Claude Cowork, Glean was preferred roughly 2.5x as often as off-the-shelf MCP tools and used 30% fewer tokens on average. Better context improves both quality and efficiency. I enjoyed talking with @CNBC's @dee_bosa about this broader shift. In enterprise AI, the winners will not be defined by better models alone. They will be defined by who builds the strongest foundation for enterprise context. Thank you to our customers, partners, and team for helping us build the future of enterprise AI.

We've talked about @Glean's Enterprise Knowledge Graph since 2019. People kept asking to see it, so we built a demo. Ask Glean something like: "show me my manager's projects and the team behind them," and watch it traverse the graph in real time, mapping work and people across the org. Very cool!

Can your AI answer these questions? - Who owns this customer escalation? - What changed on this project after last week's review? - Why did we decide to push the launch? @glean's Enterprise Graph connects people, projects, docs, and customers so AI can reason across how work actually gets done, instead of starting cold every time. More effective, less expensive. For a long time, people have asked us if they could actually see the graph. Now you can.

“AI costs are rivaling payroll costs,” @Glean founder and CEO @jainarvind tells @inafried at the #AxiosAISummit. Businesses will have to account for AI use in their hiring plans, he added. https://t.co/759riGtqsu

over the last 4 months, i built @getlindy's ugc program from 0 to 100+ creators, tens of millions of views, at ~$1 CPM. here's what i learned: 1. make everything as simple as possible when you're managing this many people, complexity kills you. onboarding should be frictionless. rules should be clear. payment structure should be simple. from the moment a creator joins to the moment their first video goes live, there should be zero friction. 2. reward your creators well there are hundreds of ugc programs out there. if you want to recruit great creators and actually keep them, you have to stand out. that starts with compensation, so pay well from the start. but it goes beyond money. random shoutouts, surprise bonuses, making them feel like they're part of something — that's what separates programs creators stay loyal to from ones they don’t care about. treat them like partners, not contractors. 3. operations is the real job i'm confident in my ability to make a viral video, but managing a ugc program means much more than just planning content. it's sourcing creators, meeting with them regularly, answering questions, managing payouts, refining guidelines, building systems — and then doing it all again the next day. the content is almost the easy part. keeping the program alive is where all your time actually goes. / in conclusion i had a blast building this program, super grateful for the team and all the creators !
Big congratulations to my friend @Altimor and the entire team at @getlindy for this incredible launch and new product. If you haven’t yet, check it out, it will change your life!

没想到宝玉 @dotey 和大辉 @Fenng 就微信编辑器的事居然吵起来了，我也说说我的看法。 大辉说编辑器看着简单其实不好做是没错的，我们当年做网易博客的时候就做过两代编辑器，后来兄弟部门做有道云笔记和灵犀文档（内部用的类似飞书）也有所参与，现在做@remio_ai 也要做编辑器，确实感觉不太好做。 我也是觉得微信公众号后台的编辑器很不好用，在这点上我和宝玉的体感很像。但这主要是因为我习惯Markdown风格的编辑器，是个人风格的原因。我也不太认同池大说的如果对排版的要求比较简单的话，微信的编辑器也OK，感觉不好用是因为对排版的要求花样多。我觉得是反的，我是觉得越简单越会觉得不好用，因为简单排版Markdown好用多了，但对排版要求高的话，微信编辑器配合插件的能力上限很高。别的编辑器搞不定的似乎微信都能搞定，因为开放了很强的裸HTML功能。 我个人觉得，微信编辑器做成这样是因为微信在设计时更注重能力上限和灵活性，这样可以让插件尽情发挥。但与此同时，也导致了官方编辑器本身提供的能力处在底层，有点毛胚房的感觉。这可能是无奈之举，如果官方编辑器自身的功能很强很丰富，就不免会带来一些结构性约束，导致插件都很难处理。 举个例子，很多插件都提供特殊的标题样式，如果官网编辑器支持Markdown，那你输入## 是不是应该自动变成插件的二级标题样式呢？这其实很不容易做到。 但是我也是不能理解为什么不支持直接粘贴图片而只能上传。以我的了解，支持粘贴图片应该没什么副作用。

自3月初发布内测版以来，经过7个月紧锣密鼓的开发，今天我们正式发布 @remio_ai 2.0版本。 3月初发布内测版时，很多人以为我们是一个笔记应用，因为首页就是一个Note列表，除了网页自动保存也不支持其他数据源，问答功能也很基础。 但这从来不是我们的定位。这7个月，我们极大增强了数据源整合能力和AI助手能力。 🧠 数据源整合，构建最全面的个人记忆 最受欢迎的是文件夹同步，再也不需要手动上传文件到ChatGPT； 网页自动保存不仅支持普通资讯网页，还能自动保存你和ChatGPT、Gemini、DeepSeek等AI的聊天记录、YouTube字幕、在线PDF、Google Docs、Twitter thread； 无限量免费录音转写——无论线上会议、现场研讨会还是线下讲座，remio都能录制、转写，并即时生成包含关键决策和行动项的AI摘要； 此外还支持导入Markdown笔记、同步Slack和邮件数据。 remio 2.0整合文件、网站、Slack、邮件等所有内容，打破信息孤岛，构建最全面的个人记忆。 🔒 local-first和BYOK提供100%的隐私保护 为此保驾护航的是local-first技术架构：所有数据在本地保存、解析和索引，只使用API调用LLM，确保数据不会用于LLM训练。你还可以使用自己的LLM key（BYOK），此时remio将完全不接触你的任何内容。 🤖 最前沿的AI助手能力 支持GPT、Gemini、Claude、DeepSeek等最前沿的LLM； 强大的意图理解引擎，听懂工作中的典型话术，精准搜索资料； 通过@针对当前文档、项目进行精准问答； 浏览器插件让问答能力触手可及； 精准到句子级别的引用来源，核实信息异常方便； Prompt Library让常见提示词方便复用。

今天OpenAI推出了自己的AI浏览器ChatGPT Atlas，显示了其过剩的资源和无处安放的精力。ChatGPT Atlas也没什么创新，约等于Dia加@remio_ai 1.0的web auto capture。 ChatGPT Atlas的Browser Memories “致敬”了remio早在3月初推出时首创的自动保存网页内容的思路，说明这个思路开始被业界认可，再次证明我们走的路是对的。 我们在七个半月前的1.0版本就提供了Browser Memories，现在我们都已经升级到2.0了。OpenAI，你落后太多啦。 而且我们认为这是用户的数据，应该归用户，所以这些数据我们都存在用户电脑本地，而不是像OpenAI一样把数据放在云上，还不能导出。 数据属于用户（及所在的企业），应该能够自由的用任何最合适的大模型来处理。用户拥有这个权利，任何把用户数据强行跟模型绑定的行为，只可能来自于资本的利益而非用户利益。 OpenAI就是因为从资本的利益出发，想要用户数据，所以Browser Memories默认是不敢打开的。

周末不到两天时间指挥AI写了差不多2000行代码了，预研了两个功能：Apple Notes笔记内容实时同步与解析、Excel文档的解析和问答。 Apple Notes同步和解析基本已经做完，搞定了最复杂的表格内容解析和附件的提取，不久将会集成到 @remio_ai 里。 Notes的表格数据的表示非常复杂，如果没有AI，不是说要多久才能实现的问题，是觉得不知道能不能实现压根就不会去干的问题。Claude Sonnet-4.5尝试了多次，始终没有解决行列乱序问题，换成GPT-5才终于解决。但附件提取GPT-5又没搞定，换成Sonnet搞定了。 Excel文档问答会更难，现有的方案虽然初步能走通，但纯靠大模型暴力回答。大模型连数数都数不清楚，还不实用。实用的方案最好是大模型、Text2SQL、Python脚本三管齐下。这方面还需要更多的实验。 用AI编程，就像建筑师在指挥施工队，施工队帮你做了很多体力活，但整个建筑还是要你设计，施工过程也需要经常把关。

这两天微信占存储空间大的事情又在中推上闹的沸沸扬扬的。@remio_ai 考虑到保护用户的数据安全和隐私（并且remio也不是社交或协作软件，没有必要数据上云），和微信一样把数据存储在本地，空间占用的问题是我们从第一天就要认真考虑的。 当前的实现肯定远非完美，但remio的关键设计都充分考虑到减少空间占用的目标，比如： 1、不保存网页的原始格式内容，只保存网页正文内容的Markdown，不自动保存X首页等内容很多的信息流，这使得重度用户每个月网页自动保存的文本数据也不过20MB。 2、不保存网页的原始图片格式，都转成中等质量的webp格式，这使得重度用户每个月网页自动保存的图片数据也不过200MB级别。 3、精细的URL去重处理，避免同一个网页保存多次。很多网页带动态参数，有很多参数不影响网页内容（如推广的utm_source, referer之类），但很多参数又会影响内容（很多SaaS是这样），所以不能一刀切处理，这个方面我们持续优化策略，目前应该基本上不会重复保存了。 4、对用户已有的本地文件不增加一份存储（所有要把文件上云的产品都增加了一份存储），而只是对文件内容做了索引。初步看索引占用的空间只有原始文件的10%左右。 5、录音使用的是m4a格式，一个小时只有10MB出头的大小。 6、邮件（这个正在开发）也只保存邮件正文的TXT格式和附件的Markdown，不会导致邮件数据又被存了一份。 后续我们还会提供智能空间管理能力。网页图片和录音是相对比较吃空间的，后续我们将会提供一键清理几个月之前且没有访问过和被引用的图片和录音的功能，这样那些很久以前没用的数据就会被清掉，那些虽然很久了但还有用的数据就会被自动保留。 remio的深度用户把一辈子的记忆都交给remio了，remio要担得起用户的信任和期望。万一哪天remio真的活不下去了，remio帮你保存、解析、转录的那些数据，都以Markdown和图片的格式存储在你电脑上，你也随时可以导入到别的类似软件里去。

Knowing your core values will help you understand why you make certain choices, recognize when something feels off, and live with more intention. When something is in conflict with one of your core values, it leads to internal conflict and relationship friction. Knowing your core values and the core values of others will help you minimize conflict because you learn their behaviors and decisions align with their core values, not yours. For example, if one of your top 5 core values is family, and your spouse doesn't have family anywhere on their list, it will help you both understand why you want to spend time with your extended family, but your spouse does not. It's simply not something they value. From this understanding, you can compromise - maybe you spend more time with family while your spouse joins for 1 out of 3 family events. There are dozens of examples. I love that it helps you quickly get to the root cause of conflict so resentment and feeling, "misunderstood,” happen less frequently. ——— And yes, I know there are other core values apps. And yes, I know mine is easier to use. 👩🏻‍💻 And yes, I’m charging $2.99 one-time for the app because I wanted to see how easy the integration with @RevenueCat would be. Someone paid $4,200 on Day 1 of @rocketdotnew 1.0. Annual plan. Solve + Build + Intelligence. No sales call. No discount. No free trial expiring. They watched the video, read what we shipped, and decided it was worth a year's commitment before the launch day was over. I sat with that for a while. Because this is a market where every AI tool is racing to be cheaper. Where growth means discounting until the unit economics break. Where "revenue marketing" means posting ARR numbers to attract more users, you'll lose in 90 days. We went the other direction. We launched a higher-priced package. On purpose. Deep agentic capabilities cost real money to build and real money to run. We'd rather charge what the value is worth than subsidize usage and call it growth. That's a choice most companies in PLG AI space won't make. We made it on day one and someone validated it on day one. Now, ever since I wrote that gross margin post a few weeks ago, my DMs have been interesting. People asking where Rocket actually stands. And now after Rocket 1.0, DMs are on Is V1.0 a sign that V0 wasn't working. Whether this is just another wrapper looking for a story. So let me be transparent. Before Rocket 1.0 even existed, the previous version hit 8-digit annualized revenue. Real subscription money in the bank, not projections. 50%+ gross margins. 35-40% month-over-month growth. $3,000 ARPU because we focused on quality over quantity from the start. We didn't build 1.0 because something was broken. We built it because the vision demanded more than what was already working. V1.0 was six months of building while the business was running. For everyone who thinks this is a wrapper, we'll be sharing the deep engineering behind these capabilities in the coming weeks. That conversation will be worth your time. You won't see revenue marketing campaigns from us. Maybe we'll share numbers occasionally. No campaigns built around them. We run this business on fundamentals, not FOMO. It's time to bring vibe into business, instead of doing vibe business. IYKYK.

Who > What. @suno just announced a well-deserved funding round at a $5.4B valuation. I was fortunate to be their sole pre-seed investor, but I have to confess, I didn’t love the music business when we invested back in 2022. At the time, crypto was king and AI was still a novelty. Music apps are a notoriously challenging category. ChatGPT hadn’t yet landed in the hands of users and showed what was possible. But this team marched to the beat of their own drum. When I met @MikeyShulman, Martin and Georg, they were a trio of wizards who understood audio and AI better than anyone on the planet. I had misgivings about the music business, but had total conviction about the team. Suno wasn’t actively fundraising, so I pitched @fcollective. Luckily Mikey thought we may be able to add some value ... It was well worth the effort. Markets evolve, products pivot, and technologies come in and out of fashion, but brilliant, authentic, agentic founders are the constant around which great startups are built. Invest in the "who,” trust that the “what” will follow.

Almost everyone has a song in their head, but very few people have the tools to bring it to life. That’s changing with @suno. @Lightspeedvp first invested in Suno’s Series B in 2024, and today we’re excited to participate in their $400M Series D. Since our initial investment, Suno has grown into a platform reshaping how people can express themselves. A simple line of text can generate an entire song. People are making songs for family birthdays and anniversaries, turning text messages into tunes for friends, transforming Slack messages into hits at company offsites. Suno is also being used in important ways to support medical treatment and healing. From therapists helping teens navigate mental health challenges through music creation, to personalized songs tied to memories and familiar voices helping elders with memory loss. The examples and possibilities are endless. Congratulations to @MikeyShulman and the entire Suno team on this milestone. Lightspeed is proud to continue to deepen our partnership.
If this was an intentional move, I think it’s a great non-announcement by @suno. Shows they don’t care about the venture theatrics, solely focused on the mission.

To this day my favorite thing about building @suno is seeing someone use the product for the very first time. You can watch in real time as something inside of them shakes loose - for many it's the first time they've felt creative in a long time, and for most it's the first time they've ever had a chance to be creative in the medium of music. There is massive latent demand for making music and feeling creative and it's only revealing itself more each day. $5.4B is just another reminder to not be surprised by just how big this can get.

Today, I walked past the Old Street roundabout on my way to a SXSW London side event, and saw @synthesiaIO on a billboard with some of the best companies Europe has ever produced. I'll admit it gave me a much-needed energy boost ahead of speaking on yet another panel about AI's problems. The billboard is part of #BuiltinEurope, a campaign @balderton has launched to tell the truth about what is happening right now in European tech. The story we've heard for years has been one of catching up. Europe could lead, the headlines said, if only it had fixed this or that. That's because building in Europe was treated as a thing you had to overcome. Now, for the first time in a long while, the opposite is true. Met with indifference at best and opposition at worst at home, European founders are forced to build companies that work everywhere from the first day, and they're doing it with style (and substance). At Synthesia we feel this in our bones. We were built in London and we have been global from the start, because being European made that the only sensible way to think. So if you have ever thought about building a company in Europe, or joining one, consider this your invitation. https://t.co/cvFiwU9dG3 is now live, with a jobs platform pulling together open roles from a thousand European startups, and a directory of the continent's best incubators for anyone ready to start something of their own. Europe is building. Come build with us.

Venture investors poured over $7 billion into voice AI startups in the first quarter of this year, far more than in any previous period. @ElevenLabs, @synthesiaIO, and @runwayml (which is known for its video models but handles large amounts of voice generation) have all raised sizable new rounds since the start of the year. The global market for voice recognition technology, expected to be worth $22 billion in 2026, is projected to nearly triple in size over the next five years.

To be clear, @synthesiaIO is proud to be part of Built in Europe and the case @balderton makes is the right one. I am less comfortable when that ambition gets folded or confused with the version of sovereignty taking shape in Brussels, where the instinct is to reduce reliance on foreign technology through procurement rules, subsidies, and a thickening layer of complex and overlapping regulation. Real sovereignty comes from building generational companies strong enough to win on open ground. Shielding them from competition only swaps one dependence for another and creates inferior products and services at higher costs. We need more of the former, and less of the latter.
